news.title: Over 1.28 lakh pending land survey cases in Maharashtra
news.para: Maharashtra faces a backlog of over 1.28 lakh land survey cases, with resolutions averaging 121 days. The state is set to appoint licensed surveyors by March 2026, establishing clear criteria for their selection and operation. Plans are also in motion to adopt a system similar to other states, where property is officially measured before land sale deeds are registered, ensuring accurate records and streamlining the process.

news.title: Haryana RERA directs BPTP to refund maintenance charges to allottee with 11% interest
news.para: Haryana RERA’s Gurugram bench has directed BPTP to refund ₹1.16 lakh collected as maintenance charges before handing over possession of a unit in Park Spacio, Sector 37D, Gurugram. The authority also awarded ₹1 lakh as compensation and ₹50,000 as litigation cost.

news.title: Protected Aravali land cleared of illegal walls, buildings in Gurugram
news.para: Authorities in Gurugram have reclaimed nearly three acres of protected Aravali land in Behrampur village, demolishing 11 illegal structures. This significant anti-encroachment drive targeted under-construction buildings and boundary walls on land vital for groundwater recharge and wildlife corridors. The action follows years of efforts to protect this ecologically sensitive region from unauthorized development and fragmentation.

news.title: Telangana HC seeks HYDRAA, HMDA stand on three-year delay in acting against lake encroachments
news.para: The Telangana High Court has demanded explanations from HYDRAA and HMDA regarding their inaction against encroachments around Errakunta lake. An advocate's petition highlighted repeated complaints over three years, alleging selective enforcement and failure to protect the water body despite its notification. The court seeks details on steps taken to safeguard the lake and prevent illegal constructions, with the next hearing scheduled for July 16.

news.title: Max Estates records ₹1,100 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Max Estates sold 487 units across its projects in Noida and Gurugram during Q1 FY27, compared to 43 units in Q1 FY26. The company achieved collections of about ₹500 crore during the quarter.

news.title: Lucknow development body nod to affordable housing plan in Anant Nagar
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority has greenlit Aditi Apartments, a significant affordable housing initiative featuring 1,392 flats in Anant Nagar. This project, designed for various income brackets, will offer 412 one-bedroom and 980 two-bedroom units.  The complex will boast five residential towers, ample parking, a substantial park, and modern amenities like rainwater harvesting and high-speed lifts, enhancing urban living.

news.title: Raymond Realty records ₹700 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Raymond Realty’s pre-sales rose 129% year-on-year to ₹700 crore in Q1 FY27, despite no new residential project launch during the quarter. Collections increased 47% year-on-year to ₹550 crore.

news.title: Domestic investors account for 57% of real estate investments in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Institutional investments in Indian real estate rose 50% year-on-year to $4.5 billion in H1 2026, according to Colliers India. Domestic investor inflows stood at $2.6 billion during the period, accounting for 57% of total investments.

news.title: ACE, Japan’s Kato Works to jointly manufacture cranes in India
news.para: Action Construction Equipment and Kato Works have formed a 50:50 joint venture, ACE Kato, to manufacture high-capacity cranes in India. The JV plans to invest about ₹200 crore in an 11-acre manufacturing facility in Haryana.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh CM launches tech-driven drive for land survey, demarcation
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath launched the Digi Rover Special Land Survey Campaign in Saharanpur, aiming to resolve over 79,000 pending land demarcation cases statewide by August 15. Utilizing advanced GNSS technology, the initiative promises more accurate, transparent, and reliable land surveys, significantly reducing disputes and litigation. This tech-driven approach replaces traditional methods, ensuring citizens access swift and efficient land-related services, bolstering good governance and protecting farmers' rights.

news.title: Maharashtra government to introduce ‘Zero Royalty’ on imported sand
news.para: Maharashtra is set to implement a 'zero royalty' policy for imported sand to alleviate shortages and boost construction. Revenue Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule announced the upcoming policy, which may extend to specific districts facing scarcity. The state is also exploring transport charges for rural road repairs and considering suction pump sand extraction in the Tapi riverbed, mirroring the Konkan region's approach.

news.title: Tamil Nadu RERA imposes graded penalties on misleading real estate ads
news.para: Tamil Nadu's real estate regulator, TNRERA, has introduced a graded penalty system to curb misleading property advertisements. From July 1, developers face fines up to ₹5 lakh for violations, with penalties tiered based on project cost. This move strengthens transparency norms, requiring mandatory display of registration numbers and QR codes, and prohibits deceptive claims. TNRERA is now actively enforcing advertising standards to protect homebuyers.

news.title: Bombay HC paves way for cluster redevelopment of MHADA's two biggest layouts in Bandra, Worli
news.para: The Bombay High Court has greenlit cluster redevelopment for Mumbai's large MHADA layouts in Bandra Reclamation and Adarsh Nagar, Worli. Dismissing petitions, the court affirmed this integrated approach as a feasible and planned method for urban growth. The decision allows for coordinated development of these older housing colonies, ensuring better infrastructure and amenities for residents.

news.title: Signature Global evaluating senior living segment
news.para: Signature Global is evaluating opportunities in the senior living segment as part of its long-term growth strategy, though no decision has been finalised. India’s senior living market is currently estimated at $1-2 billion and may attract investments of $4.8-8.4 billion by 2030, according to Savills India.

news.title: Punjab cabinet gives nod to amend land pooling policy
news.para: Punjab's land pooling policy is set for an upgrade, offering landowners enhanced benefits including larger commercial and residential plots.  Additionally, the state cabinet has waived facilitation charges for essential citizen certificates until September 30 to aid electoral roll revisions.  A significant move also includes regularizing illegal colonies with 25% construction, simplifying the process for residents and developers.

news.title: National flex workspace brands may command 25% pricing premium by FY28: Report
news.para: National coworking brands currently command a 19% pricing premium over independent operators, according to a report by myHQ. The report analysed 1,384 coworking centres across 30 cities based on five years of transaction data from more than 10,000 companies.

news.title: Telangana HC declines to halt sales in high value 400-acre Kukatpally land dispute
news.para: Telangana High Court refused to halt the sale of over 400 acres in Kukatpally, linked to GOCL Corporation and other private entities. While transactions will be subject to the final court decision, the court has directed the state government and concerned departments to file their responses within three weeks. The case involves allegations of irregularities in transferring government land to private hands, potentially causing significant revenue loss to the state.

news.title: NGT orders compensation assessment, restoration of lands affected by illegal brick kilns in NTR district
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered NTR district authorities to assess and quantify environmental damage from illegal brick kilns within three months. The directive includes restoring agricultural lands and water bodies. The tribunal also mandated the AP Pollution Control Board to levy compensation on operators for air quality deterioration and soil fertility loss, emphasizing strict regulation of such activities.

news.title: Nine of 10 buildings fail fire safety checks across Nagpur's fire stations
news.para: Nagpur's fire safety compliance is alarmingly low, with over 90% of inspected buildings in some areas violating norms. RTI data reveals widespread non-compliance across multiple fire station jurisdictions, despite regular inspections. While the fire department efficiently processes No Objection Certificates and reports no fire-related deaths, the high violation rate highlights significant enforcement gaps. This stark contrast demands immediate attention to ensure public safety.

news.title: Telangana government forms committee to probe Dharani, Bhu Bharathi irregularities
news.para: The Telangana government has formed a special committee to investigate irregularities in the Dharani and Bhu Bharathi land portals. Following the identification of security flaws and unauthorized entries, the committee will identify those responsible, take stringent action, and propose preventive measures. The National Informatics Centre has been tasked with developing a new, secure system architecture.
